headline: Anderson's arm too much for Georgia defense in Colorado upset
body: Roger Anderson made the most of his opportunities Saturday, completing 24 of 38 passes for 350 yards and three touchdowns as Colorado stunned Georgia 42-28 in a game where the Buffaloes' passing game simply overwhelmed a Bulldog secondary that couldn't find answers. Georgia's Giles matched Anderson with four touchdown passes of his own, but the math was brutal: Colorado outgained Georgia 462 to 393 total yards, and more importantly, Colorado's receivers made contested catches when it mattered while Georgia's couldn't.

The disparity was sharpest in the air. Anderson and his receivers, particularly Mecum who hauled in 89 yards despite just three catches, beat Georgia's coverage consistently downfield. Giles threw for 254 yards and spread the ball around, but without a dynamic receiving corps to generate chunk plays, Georgia leaned on its running game out of necessity. Lloyd managed just 63 yards on 11 carries, a sign that Colorado's front seven was winning battles in the trenches and forcing Georgia into predictable passing situations.

Colorado's approach was methodical. Sweeney carried the ball 13 times for 72 yards and a score, a workmanlike performance that kept chains moving and kept Georgia's defense on the field and tired. The Buffaloes forced one Georgia turnover and committed just one themselves, playing clean football while Georgia played clean football too, but without the explosive plays needed to stay in rhythm. Anderson's poise in the pocket and willingness to let plays develop downfield proved the difference.
turningPoint: Colorado's passing attack found its rhythm in the second half while Georgia's defense, already undersized against the vertical passing game, began to collapse coverage underneath, allowing Anderson to dictate tempo and force Georgia into reactive football.

unsungHero.name: Mecum
unsungHero.why: Three receptions for 89 yards is an absurd efficiency rate that set up scoring situations, yet he'll be overshadowed by Anderson's gaudy yardage totals and the team victory itself.
